title: Escontria chiotilla
taxonomy: Escontria chiotilla is classified as a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Escontria, which is a group of columnar cacti. The species E. chiotilla is native to Mexico and is known for its distinctive cylindrical stems and white or yellow flowers. Escontria chiotilla is often found in dry, rocky areas and is well adapted to its arid environment. The plant's classification is further supported by its unique characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Escontria chiotilla, also known as the chiotilla or chiotillo, is a type of cactus native to the Baja California Peninsula in Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Escontria chiotilla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Mexican herbal medicine, Escontria chiotilla is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in water to treat various ailments, including colds, fever, and skin irritations.
